notes:
Stormfront (dp7) is a late Diamond & Pearl-era set (released Nov 2008). DP-era booster = 9 cards: 1 rare-or-better, 3 uncommons, 4 commons, 1 reverse-holo (Bulbapedia booster-pack structure; guaranteed 1 rare + ~3 uncommon + 1 reverse holo since Legendary Collection). Box = 36 packs, NO ETB (vintage era). Two modeled slots:

RARE SLOT (1/pack): Distribution maps fine era rates onto this set's buckets by summing. Sourced rates -> secret-rare (3 retro Charizard-line + shiny cards): ~1:36 packs per Flipside = 0.028. lv-x (Level X): Stormfront is a LATE DP set so the more favorable 1:18 packs rate applies (Flipside: "1:36 for the first sets... 1:18 for later sets") = 0.056 (~2 per box). rare-holo: DP-era holo ~1:3 packs umbrella, net of lv-x/secret holo overlap = 0.306. rare (plain): remainder = 0.610. Sum = 1.0. Per box that is ~22 plain rares, ~11 holos, ~2 Level X, ~1 secret/shiny.

REVERSE-HOLO SLOT (1/pack): 1 reverse holofoil guaranteed per pack (Bulbapedia). Reverse appears across common/uncommon/rare faces; modeled proportional to the set's non-Level-X pool weighted toward commons: common 0.45, uncommon 0.40, rare 0.15. Sum = 1.0. (Reverse-holo Level X / secret rares do not exist, so those buckets are excluded from this slot.)

Confidence MEDIUM: rates are real community/era-average figures (Flipside rarity review + Bulbapedia structure) but there is no large per-set published pull study for Stormfront specifically. Vintage sealed boxes carry a large collector premium, so computed EV/booster will sit far below box price - expected, not a reason to inflate rates.

Available buckets honored: distribution keys are a strict subset of {rare-holo, rare, uncommon, common, lv-x, secret-rare}. Each slot sums to exactly 1.0.
